channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Por qué los brotes de ébola y hantavirus han confundido a los científicos
item.description: Las noticias sobre los más recientes brotes de virus en el mundo no solo han sido aterradoras, sino también confusas, incluso para los científicos.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

item.title: U.S. Launches Fresh Strikes in Iran, Testing Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. conducted what a U.S. official said were self-defense strikes in southern Iran on Wednesday as Trump signaled readiness for a prolonged negotiation process.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Bandar Abbas, Iran, this month. The U.S. military on Wednesday knocked down four one-way attack drones that an official said Iran had launched over the strait.

item.title: China prueba su modelo de vigilancia estatal en el extranjero
item.description: Bajo el mandato de Xi Jinping, China ha intentado exportar al mundo sus ideas sobre seguridad y ha llegado a países como las Islas Salomón.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson, Berry Wang and Adam Ferguson
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: Pescando en la costa de Honiara, en la isla de Guadalcanal, en las Islas Salomón

item.title: Bare-Knuckle Tactics and Brash Promises: How Howard Lutnick Operates
item.description: Before becoming U.S. commerce secretary, Mr. Lutnick controlled 818 companies. A review of their dealings sheds light on his freewheeling first year in government.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rothfeld and Ana Swanson

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 28, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: 4 Takeaways From The Times’s Reporting on Howard Lutnick
item.description: The New York Times examined the dealings of 818 companies controlled by the U.S. commerce secretary, documenting a history of bare-knuckle tactics that extended into his time in government.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Michael Rothfeld

media:content.media:credit: Yuri Gripas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has boasted of using the power of the federal government to benefit taxpayers with aggressive deal making.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Can Hardly Believe the Reality of L.A.’s Mayoral Race
item.description: “Mayor should not be your first job,” the host said of the former reality show star and mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt, who is polling in second place.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix

item.title: Word of the Day: risible
item.description: This word has appeared in five articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Military Says It Targeted a U.S. Base in Retaliation for Strikes
item.description: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps did not say which base it had targeted early Thursday, after U.S. strikes in the country’s south. The exchanges threaten a fragile cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Oil Prices Climb on Renewed Hostilities in Middle East
item.description: Oil prices jumped after the U.S. said it shot down four Iranian attack drones, casting doubt once again on the prospects for a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jonas Roosens/Belga, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Israel’s Isolation Is Deepening Fast. It Doesn’t Have to Be This Way.
item.description: Elections this year hold out the promise that the state can change policies in a way to recover some international good will.
item.dc:creator: Shira Efron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Over 20,000 Restaurants in New York City. Only One List.
item.description: Wesley Morris and co-chief restaurant critic, Ligaya Mishan, cherry-pick highlights from the 2026 “Best Restuarants in NYC” list.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Amy Marino, Daniele Sarti, Jadzia Erskine, John White, Felice Leon and Austin Mitchell
item.category: Best Restaurants in New York City 2026
A&A Bake & Doubles Shop (Brooklyn, NY, Restaurant)

item.title: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
item.description: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton in Texas.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: State Representative James Talarico campaigned in Houston on Wednesday.

item.title: En Europa, una ola de calor primaveral rompe récords
item.description: Las autoridades de Francia y el Reino Unido advirtieron que podrían persistir unas temperaturas “sin precedentes”, en un momento en que pocos las esperaban.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Nazaneen Ghaffar

media:content.media:description: Temperatures in London climbed to 34.8 degrees Celsius, or nearly 95 degrees Fahrenheit, on Monday.

item.title: Why Pakistan and Afghanistan Are Still Fighting
item.description: Months after Pakistan declared “open war” on Afghanistan, neither side appears ready to back down, despite China’s efforts to mediate.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Zia ur-Rehman

media:content.media:credit: Tomas Munita for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Afghan Border Protection Forces officer, left, and Pakistani Army soldiers, right, at the border crossing between the two countries, in Spin Boldak, Afghanistan, in December.

item.title: How to Watch the Scripps National Spelling Bee
item.description: Nearly 250 contestants from around the nation and the world traveled to Washington for a chance at glory and a $50,000 cash prize.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn

media:content.media:description: The winner of the Scripps National Spelling Bee receives a ceramic trophy known as the Scripps Cup.

item.title: What Plunging Pork Prices Say About China’s Economy
item.description: A key measure of inflation in China, they hit a 16-year low, driven by anemic consumer spending and an oversupply of hogs.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson

media:content.media:description: A pig farm in Taizhou, China. “Many hog farmers can no longer hold out,” Sun Haoyu, who raises hogs in Dalian, China.

item.title: Corrections: May 28, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, May 28, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Farage’s Far-Right Party Has Surged to the Fore
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, May 28, 2026.

item.title: Brownie Brittle
item.description: Brownie brittle is the perfect treat for that person in your life who loves the edges of the brownie. It’s deeply chocolaty and gives a satisfying crunch with every bite.

media:content.media:credit: Kelly Marshall for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Samantha Seneviratne. Prop Stylist: Paige Hicks.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 28, 2026
item.description: John Kugelman gives us some choices to make.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Pavel Horejsi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Strap in for today’s Thursday crossword.

item.title: Massie Visits Greene in Costa Rica for Fishing and ‘Spicy’ Political Talks
item.description: The two Republican critics of President Trump — Thomas Massie, who lost his House primary last week, and Marjorie Taylor Greene, who resigned from Congress — met up in the tropics.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: U.S. Strikes Military Sites in Iran for Second Time in 3 Days
item.description: As a potential cease-fire hangs in the balance, an official said the strikes were in self-defense, with targets including four one-way attack drones launched by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Bandar Abbas, Iran, this month. The U.S. military on Wednesday knocked down four one-way attack drones that an official said Iran had launched over the strait.

item.title: They Fought for the Voting Rights Act. Now They’re Fighting Its Unraveling.
item.description: At just eight years old, Sheyann Webb-Christburg marched for Black voting rights in Selma, alongside Martin Luther King, Jr. Now, as Southern Republicans race to redraw voting district maps before the Midterm elections, Sheyann and other Black voters are protesting what they fear is the erasure of their voices.
item.dc:creator: Bethlehem Feleke, Ben Laffin and Whitney Shefte

item.title: The A.I. Tipping Point?
item.description: A.I. agents are already being used in workplaces. But what happens when they are granted legal personhood? Yuval Noah Harari, the author of “Nexus: A Brief History of Information Networks From the Stone Age to A.I.,” explains on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Why the Liberal Narrative Is ‘Collapsing’
item.description: The historian and philosopher Yuval Noah Harari is interested in how stories shape societies. On “The Ezra Klein Show,” he describes the dominant storylines of fascism, Communism and liberalism, and why the liberal narrative is struggling to hold.

item.title: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
item.description: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: E. Jean Carroll leaving court in Manhattan in 2024. Donald J. Trump later lost an appeal of a $5 million verdict against him in the case.

item.title: California Tries to Block Trump Involvement in Election Procedures
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation that tries to stop outside officials from taking ballots or getting involved in ballot processing.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:credit: Isadora Kosofsky for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters casting ballots during the 2024 election in Los Angeles.

item.title: Uganda Closes Border with Congo Over Ebola Fears
item.description: Uganda closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo over growing concerns about the possible spread of Ebola on Wednesday. Seven confirmed cases have been reported in the Ugandan capital.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: Red Cross workers moving the body of someone who may have died from Ebola, in Kampala, Uganda, on Tuesday.

item.title: 9 Missing After Deadly Implosion at Washington State Paper Mill
item.description: At least two people were killed, and eight others were injured after a tank imploded at the Nippon Dynawave Packaging plant in Longview, Washington, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling

media:content.media:description: Community members hold photographs of loved ones during a vigil at R.A. Long Park after a fatal chemical storage tank failure in Longview, Wash.

item.title: In Flint, Cash for Pregnant Women Leads to Better Outcomes for Babies
item.description: New research suggests money with no strings attached can promote better health, but other studies have seen mixed results.
item.dc:creator: Roni Caryn Rabin

media:content.media:description: The study found that the percentage of babies born prematurely and with low birth weight started to decline after the program was established in 2024.

item.title: Escándalo FIFA: juez desestima 2 acusaciones
item.description: Hernán López, un exejecutivo de Fox, y la empresa argentina Full Play Group, eludieron los cargos tras ser acusados de haber participado en una trama para entregar dinero a representantes de la FIFA a cambio de derechos de transmisión.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Ken Bensinger

media:content.media:description: Un juez desestimó dos acusaciones, incluida una contra Hernán López, ex alto ejecutivo de Fox responsable de su negocio de retransmisiones deportivas en Latinoamérica.

item.title: White House Dismisses Outline of ‘Unofficial’ Iran Deal
item.description: Plus, hunting fish with a bow and arrow. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:description: President Trump in a cabinet meeting today.

item.title: Ferrari’s Stock Falls After It Unveils Its Latest Car
item.description: The Italian sports car manufacturer received significant backlash after it unveiled its first electric vehicle, the Luce, earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Ferrari Press Office, via AFP -- Getty Images
media:content.media:description: For help designing the fully electric car, Ferrari turned to the agency founded by Jony Ive, Apple’s former design chief, and the industrial designer Marc Newson.

item.title: David Henderson, Innovative Poet and Hendrix Biographer, Dies at 83
item.description: Part of the Black Arts Movement of the 1960s, he went on to reclaim a leading musician of the psychedelic era as a distinctly African American artist.
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams

item.title: Court Orders Customs Chief to Address Compliance on Refunding Tariffs
item.description: The surprising demand to appear at a hearing suggested new concern about the Trump administration’s efforts to repay the full $166 billion owed from illegally imposed tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm

media:content.media:description: Rodney S. Scott, the head of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, has been ordered to appear before the Court of International Trade next month.

item.title: Jill Biden’s Reaction to Biden’s 2024 Debate: ‘He’s Having a Stroke’
item.description: “I had never, ever seen Joe like that,” the former first lady told CBS News. “Before or since.”
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers

media:content.media:description: Jill Biden, the first lady, and President Biden after the presidential debate in June 2024. She understood he had performed poorly but told him that they had been counted out before.

item.title: Cease-Fires, but No Peace
item.description: A truce isn’t necessarily doomed if the missiles are still flying. Here’s why.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Condé Nast Pays Over $400,000 to 3 Journalists Fired Over Protest
item.description: The former workers were among a group of employees who confronted the company’s head of human resources about layoffs last fall.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson

media:content.media:credit: Victor Llorente for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Manhattan headquarters of Condé Nast, which reached a settlement with the NewsGuild of New York, the union representing workers the company fired.

item.title: In Utah, Measles Sickens Babies and Others Who Can’t Be Vaccinated
item.description: Many of those who can’t be vaccinated, including pregnant women and immunocompromised people, are also at high risk of serious complications.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth

item.title: F.B.I. Arrests C.I.A. Official With $40 Million in Gold Bars in His Home
item.description: The only charge lodged against David Rush is that he inflated his academic credentials and obtained military leave pay worth tens of thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The C.I.A. was “unable to locate the gold bars or significant amounts of the foreign currency,” the court papers charged.

item.title: Pope Leo’s A.I. Vision Might Not Be Strange Enough
item.description: He is both a critic and a normalizer of artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; source image by proxyminder, via Getty Images

item.title: Why Democrats Think They Can Flip Texas
item.description: With Ken Paxton defeating John Cornyn in Texas’ Republican runoff for the Senate, The New York Times’s national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains why Democrats may now see their best chance in years to flip the seat.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, June Kim and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Why Ghana Is Evacuating Hundreds of Its Citizens From South Africa
item.description: Anti-immigrant protests have increased across South Africa, drawing forceful condemnation from several African leaders.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Eden Harris and Zimasa Matiwane

media:content.media:description: A man draped in a Ghanaian flag stands among other Ghanaians being repatriated from South Africa amid a wave ‌of anti-immigrant protests, on Wednesday.

item.title: La policía escolar de Texas roció con gas pimienta, golpeó y dio descargas eléctricas a varios alumnos
item.description: La presencia de agentes en las escuelas de Texas ha aumentado como medida para prevenir tiroteos. Sin embargo, una investigación reciente del Times demuestra que recurren a tácticas de mano dura con los niños.
item.dc:creator: Clare Amari, Kristian Hernández and Asher Lehrer-Small

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: En Texas, casi 400 distritos escolares tienen sus propios departamentos de policía, más que en todos los demás estados de EE. UU. juntos. Agentes en todo el estado han sujetado, derribado y paralizado con pistolas eléctricas a alumnos.

item.title: Texas School Police Pepper-Sprayed, Tackled and Tasered Students
item.description: School officers across the state turned to heavy-handed tactics on children, often in response to minor misbehavior, our investigation shows.
item.dc:creator: Clare Amari, Kristian Hernández and Asher Lehrer-Small

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Texas is home to nearly 400 school district police departments, more than all other states combined. Officers across the state have grabbed, tackled and shocked students.

item.title: Trump Threatens Oman Over the Strait, Then Says, ‘They’ll Be Fine.’
item.description: President Trump said there would be serious repercussions if Iran and Oman move forward with a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking to reporters at the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Says He Feels No Political Pressure to Make an Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump held out hope for a peace agreement, but said high oil prices would not force his hand.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Erika Solomon, Euan Ward, Luke Broadwater and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump meeting with his cabinet at the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Los asistentes de los famosos están para complacer a sus jefes, pero ¿cuál es el límite?
item.description: El asistente de Matthew Perry le inyectó la ketamina que lo mató. Su sentencia ha hecho que algunos en esta exigente profesión reflexionen sobre esta dinámica de poder.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens

media:content.media:credit: Raymond Hall/GC Images, vía Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Un informe de la autopsia atribuyó la muerte de Matthew Perry en 2023 a los “efectos agudos” de la ketamina. El asistente personal del actor lo había encontrado inconsciente en un jacuzzi de su casa de Los Ángeles.

item.title: Kids Review ‘Sarniezz’ by Angine de Poitrine
item.description: These children have a lot of thoughts about Angine de Poitrine’s song “Sarniezz (Live on KEXP),” which has been receiving loads of attention on TikTok. Is it disturbing? Creative? Nonsensical? They discuss it all with our critic, Jon Caramanica, on this special edition of Song of the Week.
item.dc:creator: Jon Caramanica, Arjun Srivatsa and Joe Coscarelli
